name: vscode-firebird-studio
description: Firebird Studio — explore, query, and manage Firebird databases directly inside VS Code.

scripts.esbuild-build: npm run -S esbuild-base -- --sourcemap && npm run -S esbuild-mcp-server -- --sourcemap && npm run -S esbuild-notebook-renderer -- --sourcemap
scripts.install-native: npm run --prefix node_modules/node-firebird-native-api/ gyp:configure && npm run --prefix node_modules/node-firebird-native-api/ gyp:build
scripts.test:vscode-host: npm run compile && tsc -p tsconfig.suite.json --noEmitOnError false && vscode-test

commands.title: Generate Data API Spec with Copilot...

firebird.mockarooApiKey.markdownDescription: To generate mock data, please insert your [Mockaroo API](https://www.mockaroo.com/users/sign_up) key.

firebird.codeCompletion.keywords.description: Enable code completion for Firebird-specific keywords.

firebird.codeCompletion.database.description: Enable code completion for Firebird Database table and field names.

firebird.useNativeDriver.markdownDescription: [Experimental] Use native driver instead of the node based driver *Needs to have FireBird installed* (required for WireCrypt support)

firebird.enableConnectionPooling.markdownDescription: Keep idle connections open and reuse them for subsequent queries instead of reconnecting every time, improving performance on repeated queries against the same database.

firebird.connectionPool.maxSize.markdownDescription: Maximum number of idle connections retained per saved connection when `#firebird.enableConnectionPooling#` is on.

firebird.connectionPool.idleTimeoutMs.markdownDescription: How long (in milliseconds) an idle pooled connection is kept before being closed, when `#firebird.enableConnectionPooling#` is on.

firebird.profiler.pollIntervalMs.markdownDescription: How often (in milliseconds) the Live Profiler re-polls connection activity while its panel is open and visible.

enumDescriptions: Use the driver's default (Read Committed, No Record Version).
Read Committed: see the latest committed version of a row a concurrent transaction is updating, instead of waiting on or conflicting with it.
Read Committed: wait on (or conflict with) a concurrent transaction updating a row, instead of reading its prior committed version.
Snapshot (Concurrency): the whole transaction sees a consistent snapshot of the database as of its start.
Snapshot Table Stability (Consistency): like Snapshot, but takes a protected lock on every table it touches.
firebird.transaction.isolationLevel.markdownDescription: Transaction isolation level for every query/batch Firebird Studio runs. Leave empty to use the driver's own default.

firebird.transaction.lockTimeoutSec.markdownDescription: Lock wait timeout, in seconds, before a query blocked on a row/table lock gives up (`0` = wait indefinitely, Firebird's default). Only honored by the pure-JS driver (`firebird.useNativeDriver: false`) — the native driver's transaction API has no equivalent numeric option, only wait/no-wait (see `firebird.transaction.waitMode`).

firebird.transaction.readOnly.markdownDescription: Open every query's transaction as READ ONLY, rejecting any statement that tries to modify data.

enumDescriptions: Wait for a conflicting lock to clear, optionally bounded by firebird.transaction.lockTimeoutSec.
Fail immediately instead of waiting for a conflicting lock.
firebird.transaction.waitMode.markdownDescription: Whether a query that hits a lock conflict waits or fails immediately.

firebird.mcp.enabled.markdownDescription: Registers the **Firebird MCP Server**, exposing whichever connections have `firebird.database.toggleMcpExposure` turned on as read-only schema-inspection tools (`list_connections`, `get_schema`) to any MCP-compatible AI client. No connection is exposed by default even when this is on — each one needs its own explicit opt-in from its right-click menu. Requires a VS Code build with MCP server registration support; has no effect on older versions.

firebird.resultsFontSize.markdownDescription: Font size (in px) for the **Query Results** grid, mirroring vscode-mssql's `mssql.resultsFontSize`. `0` uses the webview's own default size.

firebird.resultsFontFamily.markdownDescription: Font family for the **Query Results** grid, mirroring vscode-mssql's `mssql.resultsFontFamily`. Empty uses the webview's own default font.

firebird.shortcuts.markdownDescription:
Keyboard shortcuts for actions inside the **Query Results** webview (grid editing, freeze column, copy selection as SQL) — mirroring vscode-mssql's `mssql.shortcuts`. These only take effect while a result grid has focus, and use `ctrlcmd`/`+`-joined combo syntax (e.g. `"ctrlcmd+alt+i"`, where `ctrlcmd` means Ctrl on Windows/Linux and Cmd on macOS), not VS Code's own keybindings.json syntax. Omitted keys keep their default combo; set a key to `""` to disable it. Available keys and their defaults:
- `event.toggleEditing`: `ctrlcmd+alt+g`
- `event.addRow`: `ctrlcmd+alt+n`
- `event.applyChanges`: `ctrlcmd+alt+s`
- `event.toggleFreezeColumn`: `ctrlcmd+alt+z`
- `event.copyAsInsert`: `ctrlcmd+alt+i`
- `event.copyAsInClause`: `ctrlcmd+alt+k`

firebird.isqlPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `isql` or `isql-fb` executable used by **Connect with ISQL** / **Run File with ISQL**. Leave empty to search `PATH` automatically.

firebird.showSystemObjects.description: Show a 'System Tables' folder listing Firebird's built-in RDB$ system/metadata tables under each database.

firebird.dockerPath.markdownDescription: Path to the `docker` executable, used by **Add New Connection**'s Docker option to auto-detect running Firebird containers. Leave empty to search `PATH` automatically.

firebird.logLevel.description: Logging level in the output channel.

firebird.maxTablesCount.description: The maximum table count shown in the tree view. Use 0 to show all tables.

firebird.recordsPerPage.description: Number of records per page to show in results view.

firebird.linting.enabled.description: Enable SQL linting for Firebird SQL documents.

firebird.linting.missingWhereWarning.description: Warn when UPDATE or DELETE statements are missing a WHERE clause.

firebird.linting.validateTableNames.description: Validate table names against the active database schema.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Firebird Studio
walkthroughs.description: Connect to a Firebird database and run your first query, right inside VS Code.

steps.title: Add Your First Connection
steps.description: Open the DB Explorer and connect to a Firebird server or embedded database file.

steps.title: Explore the Database Structure
steps.description: Browse tables, views, procedures, triggers, generators, and domains in the tree.

steps.title: Set the Active Database
steps.description: Tell the extension which connected database your queries should run against.

steps.title: Run Your First Query
steps.description: Open a new SQL file and run a query against the active database.

steps.title: Next Steps
steps.description: IntelliSense, snippets, mock data, and the @firebird Copilot Chat participant.

chatParticipants.name: firebird
chatParticipants.description: AI assistant for Firebird SQL databases — generate queries, optimise SQL, and get explanations

commands.name: query
commands.description: Generate Firebird SQL from a natural-language description

commands.name: optimize
commands.description: Suggest optimizations for a SQL query

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain what a SQL query does in plain English

commands.name: designSchema
commands.description: Infer a Firebird table schema (DDL) from sample data

commands.name: migrate
commands.description: Convert DDL from another database system (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle) to Firebird SQL
